title: carduus meonanthus var. ammophilus
intro: Carduus meonanthus var. ammophilus, also known as Cornish thistle or Cornish plumeless thistle, is a perennial plant in the Asteraceae family, characterized by its tall stem and distinctive purple flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, due to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Carduus meonanthus var. ammophilus are said to include relief from digestive issues, such as indigestion and bloating, as well as potential anti-cancer and antioxidant effects. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is also used in some traditional remedies, although its effectiveness and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied.
